hindi: दो लोगों के लिए भोजन बंडल

food_subgroup: Multi-component meal bundle (starters, mains, and beverages combined)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for a multi-component restaurant meal bundle for two. FOODON_00002473 (meal) is used as the closest parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00FL (composite food / mixed dish) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for a bundled multi-person restaurant set meal.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for a generic multi-component restaurant meal bundle.

serving.natural_language_portion: One meal bundle for two persons, comprising two starter dishes, two main course dishes, and two beverages, with a total estimated weight of approximately 1200 g shared between two diners.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite nutrient profile derived from USDA FoodData Central reference values for Vietnamese-style mixed meal components (rice, pho, spring rolls, grilled meats, beverages) and HPB Singapore nutrient data for analogous mixed meal sets
nutrient_source.organization: USDA FoodData Central (primary); Health Promotion Board Singapore (supporting)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a generic restaurant meal bundle for two. Nutrient values are estimated per 100 g of the combined edible portion by proportional weighting of typical Vietnamese-Singaporean restaurant meal components: steamed jasmine rice (~300 g), grilled lemongrass chicken or beef (~200 g), fresh spring rolls (~150 g), stir-fried vegetables (~150 g), and two soft drinks (~400 g total). Per-100 g values are derived from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for these components and cross-checked against HPB Singapore data for analogous mixed meal sets. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; analogous estimate based on mixed rice-based Southeast Asian meal
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a restaurant meal bundle for two. GI of 52 is estimated from the analogous mixed rice-based Southeast Asian meal profile. Steamed jasmine rice has a GI of approximately 64–68 (University of Sydney GI Database); however, the presence of protein (grilled meats), fat, dietary fibre (vegetables, spring rolls), and acidic components (lime, vinegar-based dressings typical of Vietnamese cuisine) substantially lowers the effective meal GI. A blended meal GI of 52 is consistent with published mixed-meal GI values for rice-based Asian restaurant meals in Atkinson et al. 2021 (AJCN). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (52 × 198 g carbohydrate in 1200 g bundle) / 100 ≈ 32.6 for the full bundle (not per person).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from fish sauce, soy sauce, and seasoned broths typical of Vietnamese restaurant cooking
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be cautious with this meal bundle as the estimated sodium content of approximately 310 mg per 100 g (equivalent to roughly 3720 mg sodium for the full 1200 g bundle) substantially exceeds the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; requesting reduced-sauce or sauce-on-the-side options and avoiding added condiments is strongly advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (estimated 52) and high total carbohydrate load from rice-based mains and sugar-containing beverages
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should note that while the estimated meal GI of 52 is in the low range, the total glycemic load of the full bundle is high (approximately 32.6 for two persons); substituting plain water for sweetened beverages, choosing noodle-based mains over white rice, and splitting the bundle across a longer meal period can help moderate postprandial glucose excursions.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat and high sodium from restaurant-prepared components; potential for hidden trans fats in fried starters
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be aware that this restaurant meal bundle may contain moderate saturated fat (estimated 0.9 g per 100 g) and elevated sodium; choosing grilled rather than fried starters, requesting broths and sauces on the side, and limiting portion size to one main and one starter per person can reduce cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Meal Bundle for Two (双人套餐) is a multi-component restaurant meal set designed for two diners, offered by Vietnamese-Singaporean restaurants on the field survey data platform. The bundle typically comprises two starter dishes (such as fresh spring rolls, Vietnamese salad, or crispy fried items), two main course dishes (such as pho noodle soup, lemongrass grilled chicken with jasmine rice, or stir-fried beef with vermicelli), and two beverages (such as Vietnamese iced coffee, fresh lime juice, or soft drinks). The cuisine style is Vietnamese with Singaporean adaptations, featuring aromatic herbs (lemongrass, coriander, mint), fish sauce-based seasonings, and a balance of fresh and cooked components. Nutritionally, the bundle is a moderate-energy, moderate-protein meal with a significant carbohydrate contribution from rice or noodle mains and sugar from beverages. The sodium content is a primary clinical concern due to the liberal use of fish sauce, soy sauce, and seasoned broths. The estimated glycemic index of 52 (low range) reflects the moderating effect of protein, fat, and fibre on the rice-based carbohydrate load. This meal is suitable for sharing and represents good value for money in the Singapore food delivery context. Clinically, individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease should exercise portion control, request reduced-sauce options, and substitute sweetened beverages with water.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for a generic restaurant meal bundle for two. Nutrient values are estimated by proportional weighting of typical Vietnamese-Singaporean restaurant meal components using USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data and HPB Singapore analogous meal data. The GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed literature. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component-level sources are approved and the estimation methodology is documented and defensible.
notes: 1. This record represents a generic meal bundle for two as sold by Vietnamese-Singaporean restaurants on field survey data. The specific restaurant at time of data collection was Viet'Spice - Hương vị Việt - Circular Road (field survey data, SGD 63.90). 2. Nutrient values are per 100 g of the estimated combined edible portion of the full bundle (approximately 1200 g total). 3. Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the USDA FoodData Central component sources used for this estimation. 4. Vitamin E (vitamin_e_mg) is set to -1 as it is not reported consistently across the component sources. 5. Selenium (selenium_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reported in the primary component sources. 6. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy component data and are approximate. 7. Allergen information is indicative only; actual allergen content depends on the specific restaurant preparation. 8. The halal status is not confirmed as the restaurant's halal certification was not verified at time of processing. 9. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement exists for this specific meal bundle. 10. The glycemic load of 32.6 is for the full bundle (two persons); per-person GL is approximately 16.3.
